sections.vendors: No changes to the Interactive Supplier Map (OSH contributor ID: 10061) were identified this week — no new factory additions, removals, or tier-1 disclosure updates found. Core sourcing base remains India, Sri Lanka, Turkey, Bangladesh and China, with M&S's stated practice of reviewing the map at least twice a year rather than continuously.
sections.sustainability: M&S publicly reaffirmed its 'Trusted Value' strategy for the year ahead, with quality and responsible sourcing named as the first of three guiding principles. The company reiterated that all cotton in M&S clothing now comes from more responsible sources and that 91% of polyester is from verified recycled material, targeting 100% by the end of 2026.
sections.sourcing: No reported shifts in country mix this week — India, Sri Lanka, Bangladesh, Turkey and China remain the stated core, with Bangladesh still M&S's single largest apparel sourcing market (over £1bn/year, ~60 factories). No new nearshoring, diversification, or country-exit announcements surfaced.
sections.strategy: M&S doubled down on 'Trusted Value' as its guiding fashion/home strategy for 2026 (quality/sourcing, clear pricing communication, customer support amid cost pressure). Its Nordstrom wholesale tie-up — M&S's first US physical fashion presence in two decades, spanning 30 stores with Per Una and M&S Collection ranges — continues to roll out, and the monthly 'Love That Drop' capsule programme (debut: 'Sartorial Femme') has further drops scheduled through August, October, November and December.
sections.pricing: New 'Value You Can Trust' campaign spotlights entry price points such as £30 barrel-leg jeans and £30 supersoft wool jumpers. A separate release positions M&S as the 'fastest growing retailer for families,' expanding its value range with more family-focused lines at lower prices.
sections.launches: M&S launched 'Autograph Performance,' a new menswear line built on Tech Wool and 360 Flex four-way-stretch fabric technology, including a shower-resistant, machine-washable Performance Packable Suit (£129 jacket / £70 trousers). Separately, M&S staged its first-ever fashion show on Silverstone's pit lane ahead of the F1 British Grand Prix, part of a summer campaign that began with an Ibiza catwalk show reaching 26m+ views.
sections.gossip: M&S's AGM is scheduled for 7 July 2026 in London. FY26 adjusted profit before tax fell to £671.4m (from £881.1m), with the results noting £131.3m of costs tied directly to the prior cyber incident. A final dividend of 3.0p was declared (full-year dividend 4.2p, up 16.7% year-on-year). City analyst consensus remains 'Strong Buy' (16 buy / 2 hold / 0 sell across 18 analysts), with an average 12-month price target of 431p (range 360p–500p).
soWhat: Cyber-incident costs (£131.3m) are still dragging on profit even as the dividend rises — expect M&S buying teams to stay firm on FOB pricing and lean on the 'Trusted Value' narrative, so suppliers should be ready to justify cost positions with sourcing and certification evidence.

The Nordstrom wholesale model (rather than owned US stores) signals a capital-light, incremental approach to growth — near-term volume upside for suppliers is likely modest, but the template could scale to other US retail partners if performance holds.

Monthly 'Love That Drop' capsules are now a recurring fixture (next drops August–December), confirming the faster-to-market model is structural, not a one-off — suppliers offering 8–12 week lead times and small-batch flexibility (20–35 piece capsules) remain best positioned.
Strong 'Buy' analyst sentiment despite cyber-related cost drag suggests the City is backing the turnaround, reducing near-term risk of budget cuts to sourcing or sustainability programmes.
